verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum transisset sabbatum, Maria Magdalene, et Maria Jacobi, et Salome emerunt aromata ut venientes ungerent Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διαγενομένου τοῦ σαββάτου μαρία ἡ μαγδαληνὴ καὶ μαρία ἡ τοῦ ἰακώβου καὶ σαλώμη ἠγόρασαν ἀρώματα ἵνα ἐλθοῦσαι ἀλείψωσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the *mother* of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et valde mane una sabbatorum, veniunt ad monumentum, orto jam sole.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λίαν πρωῒ τῆς μιᾶς σαββάτων ἔρχονται ἐπὶ τὸ μνημεῖον ἀνατείλαντος τοῦ ἡλίου
verses.king_james: And very early in the morning the first *day* of the week, they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicebant ad invicem: Quis revolvet nobis lapidem ab ostio monumenti?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔλεγον πρὸς ἑαυτάς τίς ἀποκυλίσει ἡμῖν τὸν λίθον ἐκ τῆς θύρας τοῦ μνημείου
verses.king_james: And they said among themselves, Who shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respicientes viderunt revolutum lapidem. Erat quippe magnus valde.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναβλέψασαι θεωροῦσιν ὅτι ἀποκεκύλισται ὁ λίθος ἦν γὰρ μέγας σφόδρα
verses.king_james: And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et introëuntes in monumentum viderunt juvenem sedentem in dextris, coopertum stola candida, et obstupuerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰσελθοῦσαι εἰς τὸ μνημεῖον εἶδον νεανίσκον καθήμενον ἐν τοῖς δεξιοῖς περιβεβλημένον στολὴν λευκήν καὶ ἐξεθαμβήθησαν
verses.king_james: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a long white garment; and they were affrighted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dicit illis: Nolite expavescere: Jesum quæritis Nazarenum, crucifixum: surrexit, non est hic, ecce locus ubi posuerunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ λέγει αὐταῖς μὴ ἐκθαμβεῖσθε ἰησοῦν ζητεῖτε τὸν ναζαρηνὸν τὸν ἐσταυρωμένον ἠγέρθη οὐκ ἔστιν ὧδε ἴδε ὁ τόπος ὅπου ἔθηκαν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ite, dicite discipulis ejus, et Petro, quia præcedit vos in Galilæam: ibi eum videbitis, sicut dixit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὑπάγετε εἴπατε τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ καὶ τῷ πέτρῳ ὅτι προάγει ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν ἐκεῖ αὐτὸν ὄψεσθε καθὼς εἶπεν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illæ exeuntes, fugerunt de monumento: invaserat enim eas tremor et pavor: et nemini quidquam dixerunt: timebant enim.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξελθοῦσαι ταχὺ ἔφυγον ἀπὸ τοῦ μνημείου εἶχεν δὲ αὐτὰς τρόμος καὶ ἔκστασις καὶ οὐδενὶ οὐδὲν εἶπον, ἐφοβοῦντο γὰρ
verses.king_james: And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any *man*; for they were afraid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surgens autem mane prima sabbati, apparuit primo Mariæ Magdalene, de qua ejecerat septem dæmonia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναστὰς δὲ πρωῒ πρώτῃ σαββάτου ἐφάνη πρῶτον μαρίᾳ τῇ μαγδαληνῇ ἀφ᾽ ἡς ἐκβεβλήκει ἑπτὰ δαιμόνια
verses.king_james: Now when *Jesus* was risen early the first *day* of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had cast seven devils.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illa vadens nuntiavit his, qui cum eo fuerant, lugentibus et flentibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκείνη πορευθεῖσα ἀπήγγειλεν τοῖς μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ γενομένοις πενθοῦσιν καὶ κλαίουσιν
verses.king_james: *And* she went and told them that had been with him, as they mourned and wept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et illi audientes quia viveret, et visus esset ab ea, non crediderunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀκεῖνοι ἀκούσαντες ὅτι ζῇ καὶ ἐθεάθη ὑπ᾽ αὐτῆς ἠπίστησαν
verses.king_james: And they, when they had heard that he was alive, and had been seen of her, believed not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc autem duobus ex his ambulantibus ostensus est in alia effigie, euntibus in villam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ ταῦτα δυσὶν ἐξ αὐτῶν περιπατοῦσιν ἐφανερώθη ἐν ἑτέρᾳ μορφῇ πορευομένοις εἰς ἀγρόν
verses.king_james: After that he appeared in another form unto two of them, as they walked, and went into the country.

verses.latin_vulgate: et illi euntes nuntiaverunt ceteris: nec illis crediderunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀκεῖνοι ἀπελθόντες ἀπήγγειλαν τοῖς λοιποῖς οὐδὲ ἐκείνοις ἐπίστευσαν
verses.king_james: And they went and told *it* unto the residue: neither believed they them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Novissime recumbentibus illis undecim apparuit: et exprobravit incredulitatem eorum et duritiam cordis: quia iis, qui viderant eum resurrexisse, non crediderunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὕστερον ἀνακειμένοις αὐτοῖς τοῖς ἕνδεκα ἐφανερώθη καὶ ὠνείδισεν τὴν ἀπιστίαν αὐτῶν καὶ σκληροκαρδίαν ὅτι τοῖς θεασαμένοις αὐτὸν ἐγηγερμένον οὐκ ἐπίστευσαν
verses.king_james: Afterward he appeared unto the eleven as they sat at meat, and upbraided them with their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they believed not them which had seen him after he was risen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit eis: Euntes in mundum universum prædicate Evangelium omni creaturæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς πορευθέντες εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἅπαντα κηρύξατε τὸ εὐαγγέλιον πάσῃ τῇ κτίσει
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui crediderit, et baptizatus fuerit, salvus erit: qui vero non crediderit, condemnabitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πιστεύσας καὶ βαπτισθεὶς σωθήσεται ὁ δὲ ἀπιστήσας κατακριθήσεται
verses.king_james: He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Signa autem eos qui crediderint, hæc sequentur: in nomine meo dæmonia ejicient: linguis loquentur novis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σημεῖα δὲ τοῖς πιστεύσασιν ταῦτα παρακολουθήσει ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου δαιμόνια ἐκβαλοῦσιν γλώσσαις λαλήσουσιν καιναῖς
verses.king_james: And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues;

verses.latin_vulgate: serpentes tollent: et si mortiferum quid biberint, non eis nocebit: super ægros manus imponent, et bene habebunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὄφεις ἀροῦσιν κἂν θανάσιμόν τι πίωσιν οὐ μὴ αὐτοὺς βλάψει, ἐπὶ ἀῤῥώστους χεῖρας ἐπιθήσουσιν καὶ καλῶς ἕξουσιν
verses.king_james: They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Dominus quidem Jesus postquam locutus est eis, assumptus est in cælum, et sedet a dextris Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ μὲν οὖν κύριος μετὰ τὸ λαλῆσαι αὐτοῖς ἀνελήφθη εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν καὶ ἐκάθισεν ἐκ δεξιῶν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: So then after the Lord had spoken unto them, he was received up into heaven, and sat on the right hand of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illi autem profecti prædicaverunt ubique, Domino cooperante, et sermonem confirmante, sequentibus signis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκεῖνοι δὲ ἐξελθόντες ἐκήρυξαν πανταχοῦ τοῦ κυρίου συνεργοῦντος καὶ τὸν λόγον βεβαιοῦντος διὰ τῶν ἐπακολουθούντων σημείων ἀμήν
verses.king_james: And they went forth, and preached every where, the Lord working with *them*, and confirming the word with signs following. Amen.
